1.description: DPO-transfer example This figure illustrates the timing in our empirical specification when we use a DPO window of 3 to 6 months prior to transfer.

2.description: Transfers and DPOs over time This figure plots the number of transfers and DPOs over the sample period. Data come from Trepp for nonagency CMBS deals originated from January 2002 to December 2007, with performance measured from September 2007 to September 2011. The vertical line demarcates September 2009, the month of the IRS rule change.

3.description: Trends in transfer frequency: Loans on watchlist versus never on watchlist This figure plots the monthly frequency of transfers (number of transfers divided by number of loans outstanding) for treatment (never on watchlist prior to August 1, 2009) and control (on watchlist at least once prior to August 1, 2009) groups. The vertical line demarcates the date of the rule change. Data come from Trepp for nonagency CMBS deals originated from January 2002 to December 2007, with performance measured from September 2007 to September 2011.

4.description: Trends in transfer frequency: Delinquent loans versus never delinquent loans This figure plots the monthly frequency of transfers (number of transfers divided by number of loans outstanding) for treatment (never 30+ days delinquent prior to August 1, 2009) and control (30+ days delinquent at least once prior to August 1, 2009) groups. The vertical line demarcates the date of the rule change. Data come from Trepp for nonagency CMBS deals originated from January 2002 to December 2007, with performance measured from September 2007 to September 2011.

5.description: Dynamic triple differences coefficients: Watchlist (control) versus no watchlist (treatment) This figure plots the quarterly triple interaction coefficients from estimating Equation (8) using the set of controls and fixed effects included in Table 5A, columns 1 through 4. The vertical line demarcates the quarter of the rule change. The excluded interaction term is 2009 quarter 3, which contains the date of the IRS rule change, such that all coefficients should be interpreted relative to 2009 quarter 3. The control group is loans that appeared on the servicer watchlist at least once prior to August 1, 2009, and the treatment group is loans that never appeared on the servicer watchlist prior to August 1, 2009. Data come from Trepp for nonagency CMBS deals originated from January 2002 to December 2007, with performance measured from September 2007 to September 2011. ∗p<.1⁠; ∗∗p<.05⁠; ∗∗∗p<.01⁠.

6.description: Dynamic triple differences coefficients: Watchlist (control) versus no watchlist (treatment), including 2009Q3 This figure plots the quarterly triple interaction coefficients from estimating Equation (8) using the set of controls and fixed effects included in Table 5A, columns 1 through 4. The vertical line demarcates the quarter of the rule change. The excluded interaction term is 2009 quarter 2. The control group is loans that appeared on the servicer watchlist at least once prior to August 1, 2009, and the treatment group is loans that never appeared on the servicer watchlist prior to August 1, 2009. Data come from Trepp for nonagency CMBS deals originated from January 2002 to December 2007, with performance measured from September 2007 to September 2011. ∗p<.1⁠; ∗∗p<.05⁠; ∗∗∗p<.01⁠.
